window_summary: This window tracks the recent fatedier/frp release stream from v0.51.3 through v0.69.1, covering configuration format changes, new proxy types, plugin additions, dashboard improvements, and protocol evolution.

chunks.title: v0.51.3
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.51.3
Tag: v0.51.3; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-08-14T04:10:03Z; created_at: 2023-08-14T04:00:26Z
Release notes:
### Features
* Support Go 1.21.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.51.3, the project adds support for Go 1.21.

chunks.title: v0.52.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.52.0
Tag: v0.52.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-10-10T09:55:49Z; created_at: 2023-10-10T09:46:20Z
Release notes:
### Features
* Configuration: We now support TOML, YAML, and JSON for configuration. Please note that INI is deprecated and will be removed in future releases. New features will only be available in TOML, YAML, or JSON. Users wanting these new features should switch their configuration format accordingly. #2521
### Breaking Changes
* Change the way to start the visitor through the command line from `frpc stcp --role=visitor xxx` to `frpc stcp visitor xxx`.
* Modified the semantics of the `server_addr` in the command line, no longer including the port. Added the `server_port` parameter to configure the port.
* No longer support range ports mapping in TOML/YAML/JSON.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.52.0, configuration now supports TOML, YAML, and JSON formats, while INI is deprecated and will be removed in future releases. New features will only be available in TOML, YAML, or JSON. The way to start the visitor through the command line changes from `frpc stcp --role=visitor xxx` to `frpc stcp visitor xxx`. The semantics of `server_addr` in the command line are modified to no longer include the port, and a `server_port` parameter is added. Range ports mapping is no longer supported in TOML/YAML/JSON.

* The Dashboard is unable to display data.
* `natHoleStunServer` is missing a default value.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.52.1, fixes include: `transport.tls.disableCustomTLSFirstByte` not having any effect, the Server API not returning data correctly, the Dashboard being unable to display data, and `natHoleStunServer` missing a default value.

chunks.title: v0.53.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.53.0
Tag: v0.53.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-12-14T13:04:05Z; created_at: 2023-12-14T12:58:39Z
Release notes:
### Features
* The new command line parameter `--strict_config` has been added to enable strict configuration validation mode. It will throw an error for unknown fields instead of ignoring them. In future versions, we will set the default value of this parameter to true to avoid misconfigurations.
* Support `SSH reverse tunneling`. With this feature, you can expose your local service without running frpc, only using SSH. The SSH reverse tunnel agent has many functional limitations compared to the frpc agent. The currently supported proxy types are tcp, http, https, tcpmux, and stcp.
* The frpc tcpmux command line parameters have been updated to support configuring `http_user` and `http_pwd`.
* The frpc stcp/sudp/xtcp command line parameters have been updated to support configuring `allow_users`.
### Fixes
* frpc: Return code 1 when the first login attempt fails and exits.
* When auth.method is `oidc` and auth.additionalScopes contains `HeartBeats`, if obtaining AccessToken fails, the application will be unresponsive.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.53.0, a new command line parameter `--strict_config` enables strict configuration validation mode, throwing an error for unknown fields. SSH reverse tunneling is supported, allowing local service exposure without running frpc, using only SSH, with limitations compared to frpc. The frpc tcpmux command line parameters support configuring `http_user` and `http_pwd`. The frpc stcp/sudp/xtcp command line parameters support configuring `allow_users`. Fixes include frpc returning code 1 when the first login attempt fails and exits, and an unresponsiveness issue when OIDC auth with HeartBeats scope fails to obtain an AccessToken.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.53.2, a fix addresses a panic in frpc during login due to closing a closed channel.

chunks.title: v0.54.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.54.0
Tag: v0.54.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-02-01T06:37:53Z; created_at: 2024-02-01T06:30:17Z
Release notes:
### Deprecation Notices
* Using an underscore in a flag name is deprecated and has been replaced by a hyphen. The underscore format will remain compatible for some time, until it is completely removed in a future version. For example, `--remote_port` is replaced with `--remote-port`.
### Features
* The `Refresh` and `ClearOfflineProxies` buttons have been added to the Dashboard of frps.
### Fixes
* The host/domain matching in the routing rules has been changed to be case-insensitive.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.54.0, using an underscore in a flag name is deprecated and replaced by a hyphen, e.g., `--remote_port` becomes `--remote-port`. The frps Dashboard adds `Refresh` and `ClearOfflineProxies` buttons. Host/domain matching in routing rules becomes case-insensitive.

chunks.title: v0.55.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.55.0
Tag: v0.55.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-03-12T09:12:08Z; created_at: 2024-03-12T09:09:27Z
Release notes:
### Notable Changes
* The minimum supported Go version has been updated to `1.22`. In the new version of Go, the default minimum supported TLS version has been changed to `TLS 1.2`.
* The default value of `--strict-config` has been changed from `false` to `true`. If your configuration file uses a non-existent configuration item or has a spelling error, the application will throw an error. This startup parameter was introduced in version `v0.53.0`. If you wish to continue using the old behavior, you need to explicitly set `--strict-config=false`.
### Features
* Proxy supports configuring annotations, which will be displayed in the frps dashboard.
### Changes
* Removed dependencies on the forked version of kcp-go and beego log, kcp-go now uses the upstream version, and golib/log replaces beego log.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.55.0, the minimum supported Go version is updated to 1.22, changing the default minimum TLS version to 1.2. The default value of `--strict-config` changes from false to true, causing errors for unknown or misspelled configuration items. Proxies support configuring annotations, which are displayed in the frps dashboard. Dependencies on forked kcp-go and beego log are removed, using upstream kcp-go and golib/log instead.

chunks.title: v0.55.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.55.1

No feature changes, just a fix for the issue of no released assets in version 0.55.0.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.55.1, there are no feature changes; it only fixes the issue of missing released assets in version 0.55.0.

chunks.title: v0.56.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.56.0
Tag: v0.56.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-03-21T10:06:12Z; created_at: 2024-03-21T09:41:43Z
Release notes:
### Features
* Support range ports mapping in TOML/YAML/JSON configuration file by using go template syntax.
For example:
```
{{- range $_, $v := parseNumberRangePair "6000-6006,6007" "6000-6006,6007" }}
[[proxies]]
name = "tcp-{{ $v.First }}"
type = "tcp"
localPort = {{ $v.First }}
remotePort = {{ $v.Second }}
{{- end }}
```
This will create 8 proxies such as `tcp-6000, tcp-6001, ... tcp-6007`.
* Health check supports custom request headers.
* Enable compatibility mode for the Android system to solve the issues of incorrect log time caused by time zone problems and default DNS resolution failures.
### Fixes
* Fix the issue of incorrect interval time for rotating the log by day.
* Disable quic-go's ECN support by default. It may cause issues on certain operating systems.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.56.0, range ports mapping is supported in TOML/YAML/JSON configuration files using go template syntax. Health check supports custom request headers. Compatibility mode for Android is enabled to fix incorrect log time due to time zone problems and default DNS resolution failures. Fixes include incorrect interval time for rotating the log by day and disabling quic-go's ECN support by default due to issues on certain operating systems.

chunks.title: v0.57.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.57.0
Tag: v0.57.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-04-09T03:52:37Z; created_at: 2024-04-09T03:46:58Z
Release notes:
### Features
* `https2http` and `https2https` plugin now supports `X-Forwared-For` header.
### Fixes
* `X-Forwared-For` header is now correctly set in the request to the backend server for proxy type http.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.57.0, the `https2http` and `https2https` plugins now support the `X-Forwarded-For` header. The `X-Forwarded-For` header is now correctly set in requests to the backend server for proxy type http.

chunks.title: v0.58.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.58.0
Tag: v0.58.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-05-07T10:15:36Z; created_at: 2024-05-07T10:09:24Z
Release notes:
### Notable Changes
We have optimized the heartbeat mechanism when tcpmux is enabled (enabled by default). The default value of `heartbeatInterval` has been adjusted to -1. This update ensures that when tcpmux is active, the client does not send additional heartbeats to the server. Since tcpmux incorporates its own heartbeat system, this change effectively reduces unnecessary data consumption, streamlining communication efficiency between client and server.
When connecting to frps versions older than v0.39.0 might encounter compatibility issues due to changes in the heartbeat mechanism. As a temporary workaround, setting the `heartbeatInterval` to 30 can help maintain stable connectivity with these older versions. We recommend updating to the latest frps version to leverage full functionality and improvements.
### Features
* Show tcpmux proxies on the frps dashboard.
* `http` proxy can modify the response header. For example, `responseHeaders.set.foo = "bar"` will add a new header `foo: bar` to the response.
### Fixes
* When an HTTP proxy request times out, it returns 504 instead of 404 now.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.58.0, the heartbeat mechanism is optimized when tcpmux is enabled, with the default `heartbeatInterval` adjusted to -1 to avoid additional heartbeats. Compatibility issues may arise with frps versions older than v0.39.0; setting `heartbeatInterval` to 30 can help. Tcpmux proxies are now shown on the frps dashboard. HTTP proxy can modify response headers, e.g., `responseHeaders.set.foo = "bar"`. HTTP proxy requests that time out now return 504 instead of 404.

chunks.title: v0.58.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.58.1
Tag: v0.58.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-05-31T06:41:10Z; created_at: 2024-05-31T06:35:08Z
Release notes:
### Fixes
* Fixed an issue where HTTP/2 was not enabled for https2http and https2https plugins.
* Fixed the issue where the default values of INI configuration parameters are inconsistent with other configuration formats.
### Changes
* Updated the default value of `transport.tcpMuxKeepaliveInterval` from 60 to 30.
* On the Android platform, the Google DNS server is used only when the default DNS server cannot be obtained.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.58.1, fixes include HTTP/2 not being enabled for https2http and https2https plugins, and inconsistent default values of INI configuration parameters compared to other formats. The default value of `transport.tcpMuxKeepaliveInterval` is changed from 60 to 30. On Android, the Google DNS server is used only when the default DNS server cannot be obtained.

chunks.title: v0.59.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.59.0
Tag: v0.59.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-07-09T03:04:23Z; created_at: 2024-07-09T02:55:43Z
Release notes:
### Features
* Added a new plugin "http2http" which allows forwarding HTTP requests to another HTTP server, supporting options like local address binding, host header rewrite, and custom request headers.
* Added `enableHTTP2` option to control whether to enable HTTP/2 in plugin https2http and https2https, default is true.
### Changes
* Plugin https2http & https2https: return 421 `Misdirected Request` if host not match sni.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.59.0, a new plugin "http2http" is added, forwarding HTTP requests to another HTTP server with options like local address binding, host header rewrite, and custom request headers. An `enableHTTP2` option controls HTTP/2 in plugins https2http and https2https, defaulting to true. Plugins https2http and https2https return 421 Misdirected Request if the host does not match SNI.

chunks.title: v0.60.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.60.0
Tag: v0.60.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-08-19T05:54:34Z; created_at: 2024-08-19T05:48:17Z
Release notes:
### Features
* Added a new plugin `tls2raw`: Enables TLS termination and forwarding of decrypted raw traffic to local service.
* Added a default timeout of 30 seconds for the frpc subcommands to prevent commands from being stuck for a long time due to network issues.
### Fixes
* Fixed the issue that when `loginFailExit = false`, the frpc stop command cannot be stopped correctly if the server is not successfully connected after startup.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.60.0, a new plugin `tls2raw` enables TLS termination and forwarding of decrypted raw traffic to local service. A default timeout of 30 seconds is added for frpc subcommands to prevent commands from being stuck due to network issues. A fix addresses the frpc stop command not working correctly when `loginFailExit = false` and the server is not successfully connected after startup.

chunks.title: v0.61.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.61.0
Tag: v0.61.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-10-17T09:36:20Z; created_at: 2024-10-17T09:28:42Z
Release notes:
### Features
* The frpc visitor command-line parameter adds the `--server-user` option to specify the username of the server-side proxy to connect to.
* Support multiple frpc instances with different subjects when using oidc authentication.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.61.0, the frpc visitor command-line parameter adds the `--server-user` option to specify the username of the server-side proxy to connect to. Multiple frpc instances with different subjects are supported when using OIDC authentication.

chunks.title: v0.61.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.61.1
Tag: v0.61.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-12-16T11:48:31Z; created_at: 2024-12-16T11:41:57Z
Release notes:
### Features
* `tzdata` is installed by default in the container image, and the time zone can be set using the `TZ` environment variable.
* The `quic-bind-port` command line parameter is supported in frps, which specifies the port for accepting frpc connections using the QUIC protocol.
* The vhost HTTP proxy of frps supports the h2c protocol.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.61.1, `tzdata` is installed by default in the container image, and the time zone can be set using the `TZ` environment variable. The `quic-bind-port` command line parameter is supported in frps, specifying the port for accepting frpc connections using the QUIC protocol. The vhost HTTP proxy of frps supports the h2c protocol.

chunks.title: v0.61.2
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.61.2
Tag: v0.61.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-03-07T09:40:58Z; created_at: 2025-03-07T09:27:48Z
Release notes:
### Features
* Support metadatas and annotations in frpc proxy commands.
### Fixes
* Properly release resources in service.Close() to prevent resource leaks when used as a library.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.61.2, support is added for metadatas and annotations in frpc proxy commands. A fix properly releases resources in service.Close() to prevent resource leaks when used as a library.

chunks.title: v0.62.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.62.0
Tag: v0.62.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-04-16T08:30:12Z; created_at: 2025-04-16T08:21:46Z
Release notes:
### Notes
*   **Feature Gates Introduced:** This version introduces a new experimental mechanism called Feature Gates. This allows users to enable or disable specific experimental features before they become generally available. Feature gates can be configured in the `featureGates` map within the configuration file.
*   **VirtualNet Feature Gate:** The first available feature gate is `VirtualNet`, which enables the experimental Virtual Network functionality (currently in Alpha stage).
### Features
*   **Virtual Network (VirtualNet):** Introduce experimental virtual network capabilities (Alpha). This allows creating a TUN device managed by frp, enabling Layer 3 connectivity between different clients within the frp network. Requires root/admin privileges and is currently supported on Linux and macOS. Configuration is done via the `virtualNet` section and the `virtual_net` plugin. Enable with feature gate `VirtualNet`. **Note: As an Alpha feature, configuration details may change in future releases.**
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.62.0, Feature Gates are introduced as an experimental mechanism to enable or disable specific experimental features. The first feature gate is `VirtualNet`, enabling experimental Virtual Network functionality (Alpha). VirtualNet allows creating a TUN device managed by frp, enabling Layer 3 connectivity between different clients within the frp network, requiring root/admin privileges and supported on Linux and macOS. Configuration is via the `virtualNet` section and the `virtual_net` plugin.

chunks.title: v0.62.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.62.1
Tag: v0.62.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-04-28T06:32:27Z; created_at: 2025-04-28T06:25:15Z
Release notes:
### Bug Fixes
*   **VirtualNet:** Resolved various issues related to connection handling, TUN device management, and stability in the virtual network feature.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.62.1, various issues related to connection handling, TUN device management, and stability in the virtual network feature are resolved.

chunks.title: v0.63.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.63.0
Tag: v0.63.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-06-25T04:15:29Z; created_at: 2025-06-25T03:54:23Z
Release notes:
## Features
* Support for YAML merge functionality (anchors and references with dot-prefixed fields) in strict configuration mode without requiring `--strict-config=false` parameter.
* Support for proxy protocol in UDP proxies to preserve real client IP addresses.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.63.0, YAML merge functionality (anchors and references with dot-prefixed fields) is supported in strict configuration mode without requiring `--strict-config=false`. Proxy protocol is supported in UDP proxies to preserve real client IP addresses.

chunks.title: v0.64.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.64.0
Tag: v0.64.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-08-10T15:34:48Z; created_at: 2025-08-10T15:27:14Z
Release notes:
## Features
* Support tokenSource for loading authentication tokens from files.
## Fixes
* Fix SSH tunnel gateway incorrectly binding to proxyBindAddr instead of bindAddr, which caused external connections to fail when proxyBindAddr was set to 127.0.0.1.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.64.0, `tokenSource` is supported for loading authentication tokens from files. A fix addresses the SSH tunnel gateway incorrectly binding to proxyBindAddr instead of bindAddr, causing external connections to fail when proxyBindAddr was set to 127.0.0.1.

chunks.title: v0.65.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.65.0
Tag: v0.65.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-09-25T12:32:15Z; created_at: 2025-09-25T12:24:08Z
Release notes:
## Features
* Add NAT traversal configuration options for XTCP proxies and visitors. Support disabling assisted addresses to avoid using slow VPN connections during NAT hole punching.
* Enhanced OIDC client configuration with support for custom TLS certificate verification and proxy settings. Added `trustedCaFile`, `insecureSkipVerify`, and `proxyURL` options for OIDC token endpoint connections.
* Added detailed Prometheus metrics with `proxy_counts_detailed` metric that includes both proxy type and proxy name labels, enabling monitoring of individual proxy connections instead of just aggregate counts.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.65.0, NAT traversal configuration options are added for XTCP proxies and visitors, supporting disabling assisted addresses to avoid slow VPN connections during NAT hole punching. OIDC client configuration is enhanced with support for custom TLS certificate verification and proxy settings, adding `trustedCaFile`, `insecureSkipVerify`, and `proxyURL` options. Detailed Prometheus metrics are added with `proxy_counts_detailed` metric including proxy type and proxy name labels.

chunks.title: v0.66.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.66.0
Tag: v0.66.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-01-04T07:04:03Z; created_at: 2026-01-04T06:55:43Z
Release notes:
## Features
* HTTPS proxies now support load balancing groups. Multiple HTTPS proxies can be configured with the same `loadBalancer.group` and `loadBalancer.groupKey` to share the same custom domain and distribute traffic across multiple backend services, similar to the existing TCP and HTTP load balancing capabilities.
* Individual frpc proxies and visitors now accept an `enabled` flag (defaults to true), letting you disable specific entries without relying on the global `start` list—disabled blocks are skipped when client configs load.
* OIDC authentication now supports a `tokenSource` field to dynamically obtain tokens from external sources. You can use `type = "file"` to read a token from a file, or `type = "exec"` to run an external command (e.g., a cloud CLI or secrets manager) and capture its stdout as the token. The `exec` type requires the `--allow-unsafe=TokenSourceExec` CLI flag for security reasons.
## Improvements
* **VirtualNet**: Implemented intelligent reconnection with exponential backoff. When connection errors occur repeatedly, the reconnect interval increases from 60s to 300s (max), reducing unnecessary reconnection attempts. Normal disconnections still reconnect quickly at 10s intervals.
## Fixes
* Fix deadlock issue when TCP connection is closed. Previously, sending messages could block forever if the connection handler had already stopped.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.66.0, HTTPS proxies support load balancing groups, allowing multiple HTTPS proxies with the same `loadBalancer.group` and `loadBalancer.groupKey` to share a custom domain and distribute traffic. Individual frpc proxies and visitors accept an `enabled` flag to disable specific entries without relying on the global `start` list. OIDC authentication supports a `tokenSource` field to dynamically obtain tokens from external sources, with `type = "file"` or `type = "exec"` (requiring `--allow-unsafe=TokenSourceExec`). VirtualNet implements intelligent reconnection with exponential backoff. A deadlock issue when TCP connection is closed is fixed.

chunks.title: v0.67.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.67.0
Tag: v0.67.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-01-31T06:12:29Z; created_at: 2026-01-31T06:02:37Z
Release notes:
## Features
* frpc now supports a `clientID` option to uniquely identify client instances. The server dashboard displays all connected clients with their online/offline status, connection history, and metadata, making it easier to monitor and manage multiple frpc deployments.
* Redesigned the frp web dashboard with a modern UI, dark mode support, and improved navigation.
## Fixes
* Fixed UDP proxy protocol sending header on every packet instead of only the first packet of each session.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.67.0, frpc supports a `clientID` option to uniquely identify client instances, with the server dashboard displaying all connected clients with online/offline status, connection history, and metadata. The frp web dashboard is redesigned with a modern UI, dark mode support, and improved navigation. A fix addresses UDP proxy protocol sending header on every packet instead of only the first packet of each session.

chunks.title: v0.68.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.68.0
Tag: v0.68.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-03-20T06:05:18Z; created_at: 2026-03-20T05:56:52Z
Release notes:
## Features
* Added a built-in `store` capability for frpc, including persisted store source (`[store] path = "..."`), Store CRUD admin APIs (`/api/store/proxies*`, `/api/store/visitors*`) with runtime reload, and Store management pages in the frpc web dashboard.
## Improvements
* Kept proxy/visitor names as raw config names during completion; moved user-prefix handling to explicit wire-level naming logic.
* Added `noweb` build tag to allow compiling without frontend assets. `make build` now auto-detects missing `web/*/dist` directories and skips embedding, so a fresh clone can build without running `make web` first. The dashboard gracefully returns 404 when assets are not embedded.
* Improved config parsing errors: for `.toml` files, syntax errors now return immediately with parser position details (line/column when available) instead of falling through to YAML/JSON parsing, and TOML type mismatches report field-level errors without misleading line numbers.
* OIDC auth now caches the access token and refreshes it before expiry, avoiding a new token request on every heartbeat. Falls back to per-request fetch when the provider omits `expires_in`.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.68.0, a built-in `store` capability is added for frpc, including persisted store source, Store CRUD admin APIs with runtime reload, and Store management pages in the frpc web dashboard. Proxy/visitor names are kept as raw config names during completion. A `noweb` build tag allows compiling without frontend assets. Config parsing errors are improved for TOML files. OIDC auth caches the access token and refreshes it before expiry.

chunks.title: v0.68.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.68.1
Tag: v0.68.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-04-13T17:37:42Z; created_at: 2026-04-13T17:29:14Z
Release notes:
## Fixes
* Fixed a configuration-dependent authentication bypass in `type = "http"` proxies when `routeByHTTPUser` is used together with `httpUser` / `httpPassword`. This affected proxy-style requests. Proxy-style authentication failures now return `407 Proxy Authentication Required`.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.68.1, a fix addresses a configuration-dependent authentication bypass in `type = "http"` proxies when `routeByHTTPUser` is used together with `httpUser` / `httpPassword`. Proxy-style authentication failures now return 407 Proxy Authentication Required.

chunks.title: v0.69.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.69.0
Tag: v0.69.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-05-21T17:11:56Z; created_at: 2026-05-21T17:02:49Z
Release notes:
## Compatibility Policy
Starting with v0.69.0, each minor release is supported until there are nine newer minor releases. For example, v0.69.0 will be supported until v0.78.0 is released. Within this window, frpc v0.69.0 is guaranteed to work with any frps from v0.61.0 to v0.77.0, and vice versa. Patch releases within the same minor are always compatible. Versions outside the support window may continue to work on a best-effort basis, but compatibility is no longer guaranteed.
For mixed-version deployments, upgrade frps first, then upgrade frpc. This keeps the server side ready for newer client-side protocol behavior before clients start using it.
## Notes
This release introduces wire protocol v2 as a transition path for future frpc/frps protocol changes. The existing wire protocol is difficult to extend without compatibility risk, and upcoming changes, including replacing deprecated stream encryption methods, require a versioned protocol.
**The default value of `transport.wireProtocol` remains `v1` in this release.** Users can keep the default for now. To test v2 early, upgrade both frpc and frps to versions that support it, then set `transport.wireProtocol = "v2"` in frpc. A v2-enabled frpc cannot connect to an older frps.
When `transport.wireProtocol = "v2"` is enabled, the control channel uses negotiated AEAD encryption after the login handshake. Both frpc and frps must be upgraded to this release to use v2.
v1 will be deprecated when v2 becomes the default in a future release. It will continue to be supported until v0.78.0 is released, and may be removed in v0.78.0 or later.
## Features
* Added `transport.wireProtocol` for frpc to select the internal message protocol used between frpc and frps. Supported values are `v1` and `v2`.
* Added client protocol visibility in the frps dashboard and `/api/clients` API. Online clients now report their negotiated protocol as `v1` or `v2`.
* Wire protocol v2 now negotiates AEAD control-channel encryption. Supported algorithms are `xchacha20-poly1305` and `aes-256-gcm`; frpc advertises its preferred order based on local AES-GCM hardware support, and frps selects the first supported algorithm from that list.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.69.0, a compatibility policy is introduced: each minor release is supported until there are nine newer minor releases. Wire protocol v2 is introduced as a transition path for future protocol changes, with the default remaining v1. When v2 is enabled, the control channel uses negotiated AEAD encryption after the login handshake. Supported algorithms are xchacha20-poly1305 and aes-256-gcm. Client protocol visibility is added in the frps dashboard and API.

chunks.title: v0.69.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: fatedier/frp
Release: v0.69.1
Tag: v0.69.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-06-01T10:17:17Z; created_at: 2026-06-01T10:07:20Z
Release notes:
## Features
* `transport.wireProtocol = "v2"` now also applies to UDP-based proxy payloads, including ordinary UDP and SUDP, so their payload framing is consistent with the selected wire protocol.
* Improved SUDP compatibility during mixed `transport.wireProtocol` deployments, allowing frps to bridge payloads between v1/default and v2 SUDP clients.
* XTCP work connection `NatHoleSid` messages now follow the selected `transport.wireProtocol`.
## Compatibility Notes
* When enabling `transport.wireProtocol = "v2"` for SUDP, upgrade both the proxy and visitor frpc instances first, or keep them on `v1` until both sides are upgraded.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the fatedier/frp release v0.69.1, `transport.wireProtocol = "v2"` now applies to UDP-based proxy payloads, including ordinary UDP and SUDP. SUDP compatibility is improved during mixed wire protocol deployments. XTCP work connection `NatHoleSid` messages follow the selected wire protocol. When enabling v2 for SUDP, both proxy and visitor frpc instances should be upgraded first.

questions.query_text: In the fatedier/frp releases, which release introduces the `--strict_config` command line parameter to enable strict configuration validation mode?

answer_support.answer_text: a new command line parameter `--strict_config` enables strict configuration validation mode

questions.query_text: Across the fatedier/frp releases, how does the configuration format change described in v0.52.0 differ from the strict configuration behavior change described in v0.55.0?

options.text: v0.52.0 adds TOML/YAML/JSON support and deprecates INI, while v0.55.0 changes the default of --strict-config from false to true.
v0.52.0 removes range ports mapping, while v0.55.0 adds range ports mapping back.
Both releases only update the minimum Go version without configuration changes.
v0.52.0 introduces --strict-config, while v0.55.0 removes it.

The default value of `--strict-config` changes from false to true
